Etymology (Simple English): From 'holla' (shout or call). It means 'listen to me' or 'hi'.
Short for 'God be with ye' (God be with you). A wish for a safe trip.
From 'hy' or 'hey'. A sound to get someone's attention.
From 'good' and 'morning' (early day). A wish for a good start to the day.
From 'good', 'after' (later), and 'noon' (middle of the day). A greeting for the middle of the day.
From 'good' and 'evening' (end of the day). A greeting for the late part of the day.
From 'good' and 'night' (dark time). Said before going to sleep.
From old word 'thanc' (good thought). It means 'I have good thoughts for you'.
A short, everyday way to say 'thank you'.
Short for 'if it please you' (if it makes you happy). Used to be polite.
From 'oll korrect', a funny spelling of 'all correct' (everything is right).
From old words meaning 'so be it' (let it be this way). Used to agree.
From old words meaning 'not ever'. Used to disagree.

A1 Example Sentence: I am sorry, I am late.
Etymology (Simple English): From old word 'sarig' (sad or sore). It means 'I feel sad about this'.
From 'wil' (wish or happy) and 'cuma' (guest). A happy greeting for a guest.
